sections.instruction: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.

questions.content: 1. {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C for Questions 11-14. Choose TWO letters A-E for Questions 15-16, 17-18 and 19-20.

questions.content: According to the speaker, why is it a good time for D-I-Y painting?
options: A. there are better products available now
B. materials cost less than they used to
C. people have more free time than before

questions.content: What happened in 2009 in the UK?
options: A. a record volume of paint was sold
B. a large amount of paint was wasted
C. there was a major project to repaint public buildings

questions.content: What does the speaker say about paint quantity?
options: A. it is not necessary to have exact room measurements
B. it is better to overestimate than to underestimate
C. an automatic calculator can be downloaded from the internet

questions.content: What does Community RePaint do?
options: A. it paints people’s houses without payment
B. it collects unwanted paint and gives it away
C. it sells unused paint and donates the money to charity

questions.content: What TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about paint?
options: A. do not buy expensive paint
B. test the color before buying a lot
C. choose a light color
D. use water-based paint
E. buy enough paint for more than one application

questions.content: What TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about preparation?
options: A. replace any loose plaster
B. don’t spend too long preparing surfaces
C. use decorators’ soap to remove grease from walls
D. wash dirty walls with warm water
E. paint over cracks and small holes

questions.content: What TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about painting?
options: A. put a heater in the room
B. wash brushes in cold water
C. use a roller with a short pile
D. apply paint directly from the tin
E. open doors and windows

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C for Questions 21-26. For Questions 27-30, choose FOUR answers from the list and write the correct letter next to each book.

questions.content: Why is Matthew considering a student work placement?
options: A. he was informed about an interesting vacancy
B. he needs some extra income
C. he want to try out a career option

questions.content: Which part of the application process did Linda find most interesting?
options: A. the psychometric test
B. the group activity
C. the individual task

questions.content: During her work placement, Linda helped find ways to
options: A. speed up car assembly
B. process waste materials
C. calculate the cost of design faults

questions.content: Why did Linda find her work placement tiring?
options: A. she was not used to full time work
B. the working hours were very long
C. she felt she had to prove her worth

questions.content: What did Linda’s employers give her formal feedback on?
options: A. engineering ability
B. organizational skills
C. team working

questions.content: What was the main benefit of Linda’s work placement?
options: A. improved academic skills
B. an offer of work
C. the opportunity to use new software

questions.content: What does Linda think about the books on Matthew’s reading list?
options: A. helpful illustrations
B. easy to understand
C. up to date
D. comprehensive
E. specialized
F. useful case studies
27. The Science of Materials
28. Materials Engineering
29. Engineering Basics
30. Evolution of Materials

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Animal hides – two types

Parchment
Sheep skin: white in color and {{BLANK}}
Greasy – writing cannot be erased so often used for {{BLANK}}

Vellum
Calf skin: most popular for prestigious work because you can get {{BLANK}} lettering

Preparation of hides:
• Treated in berries of lime – where this was not available skins were {{BLANK}} (removed hair)
• Stretched tight on a frame
• Scraped to create same {{BLANK}}
• Vellum was {{BLANK}} – for correct color

Genetic testing – finding origins
Previously – analysed handwriting and {{BLANK}} used by the writer
Now – using genetic data from ‘known manuscripts’ to create a {{BLANK}}

Uses of new data
Gives information on individual books
Shows the {{BLANK}} of the book industry
Helps define {{BLANK}} in medieval period
